paper_title: Paleoclimatic potential of dendroanatomy in Picea engemannii

methods.method_name: Statistical Analysis of Tree Ring Data
methods.description: Statistical procedures used to evaluate the strength of the common signal between tree ring series and to assess the degree of co-variation among various parameters.

steps.description: To evaluate the strength of the between-series common signal and establish the replication needed to obtain mean chronologies meeting the commonly accepted standard, we used the RBAR (defined as the mean Pearson’s correlation coefficient between all possible pairs of individual tree ring series) and expressed population signal (EPS) statistics.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate RBAR and EPS statistics for tree ring series

steps.description: To assess the degree to which the various parameters co-vary, principal component analysis (PCA) and pairwise correlations were computed over the 1700–1994 period.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA and compute pairwise correlations for parameters

methods.description: Analysis to evaluate the potential risk of resolution bias in dendroanatomy parameters and their correlation with climate data.

steps.description: We have correlated the percentile MXBI chronologies against the target July–August Tmax and against the full detrended original ring width chronology, using resampling of data.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate MXBI chronologies with July–August Tmax and ring width chronology

steps.description: Calibrations are performed against July–August maximum temperatures using 1000 calibration trials where parameter chronologies are built from 10 series randomly drawn without replacement from the sample cohort.
steps.searchable_summary: perform calibration trials against July–August Tmax using random sampling
